description: LabCorp end-to-end scenarios (Phase 2b — Chunk 7). Each scenario points to one of the fixtures under server/integrations/labcorp/mock-responses/ and declares the codes + abnormal flags the LabSynthesisAgent must surface. Consumed by run-tests.js LabCorp Chunk 7 tests — adding a scenario here extends the test suite automatically, no harness changes required.

contract.fixture: Filename (relative to server/integrations/labcorp/mock-responses/) — XML in the LabCorpResult envelope that parser.js recognizes.
contract.external_order_id: Opaque LabCorp-issued order id. Used only for traceability in logs and LAB_SYNTHESIS_READY payloads — does not drive fixture selection.
contract.expected.expected_codes: Every code (or displayName) that MUST be present in the parsed output. Subset check — parser may return additional rows.
contract.expected.abnormal_flags: Map of code -> expected abnormalFlag ('H', 'HH', 'L', 'LL', 'N'). Strict equality check per row.

scenarios.name: CBC with erythrocytosis — polycythemia signal relevant to testosterone monitoring
scenarios.clinical_relevance: Erythrocytosis (HCT > 54%) is the most common dose-limiting adverse event of testosterone replacement therapy. LabSynthesisAgent must surface this so DomainLogicAgent's hrt-tt-hct-high safety rule can block further titration.

scenarios.name: Comprehensive Metabolic Panel with kidney dysfunction signal
scenarios.clinical_relevance: BUN elevated, creatinine elevated, eGFR low — classic CKD stage 3a picture. Downstream CDS should flag renal dosing considerations before any medication adjustment.

scenarios.name: Low testosterone — male hormone workup ready for HRT rule evaluation
scenarios.panel_label: Male Hormone Panel — Total T, Free T, SHBG, PSA
scenarios.clinical_relevance: Total testosterone 198 ng/dL, free testosterone 4.1 pg/mL — both below reference range. Feeds Phase 1b's hrt-tt-low-male rule, which proposes testosterone cypionate initiation gated by Tier 3 physician approval. Standard-of-care guardrail: Phase 1b DomainLogicAgent only proposes treatment if mainstream CDS raises no contraindication.

scenarios.name: Hemoglobin A1c — uncontrolled diabetes signal
scenarios.clinical_relevance: A1c 8.2% with eAG 189 mg/dL indicates suboptimal glycemic control. Relevant to both mainstream CDS (intensify antihyperglycemic regimen) and DomainLogicAgent's insulin-resistance functional-med rule.

scenarios.name: TSH elevated with positive TPO antibodies — Hashimoto pattern
scenarios.panel_label: Thyroid Panel — TSH, Free T4, Free T3, TPO Ab
scenarios.clinical_relevance: Subclinical hypothyroidism with positive TPO antibodies is the classic Hashimoto's presentation. Feeds the functional-med Hashimoto rule; mainstream CDS evaluates whether levothyroxine initiation is indicated per ATA guidance.
